data.body:
COMPLETION VERIFICATION (read-only, re-run this session) — VERDICT: FAIL.

Verified each child's compiled state via show_ticket against the live tree. The epic's PRIMARY acceptance criterion is "All six child stories (S1-S6) closed."

Child status (authoritative):
- S1 0250-36d4-c97f-47b4 (make-fawn-box, runner-seam caching): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S2 c6e5-9936-d07c-4b16 (pale-fist-prow, engine-wide caching): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S3 ba7e-dd8d-7cac-453f (vale-noun-tumor, parallelize + warm-then-fan-out + budget cap): status=in_progress, signature=null.
- S4 98c6-81c7-175e-416f (run-atom-drawl, merge G3+G4): status=OPEN, signature=null.
- S5 1762-9641-993a-464f (adept-pen-flame, child bin-packing): status=OPEN, signature=null.
- S6 da34-ac98-e375-4af0 (safe-peg-tuner, fidelity-eval harness): status=in_progress, signature=null.

=> 0 of 6 children closed (2 open, 4 in_progress); none signed by the completion-verifier gate. AC1 is decisively unmet, and the epic itself is status=in_progress.

Other ACs not demonstrable as closed deliverables: the eval-gated children S4/S5 are unstarted (open), so "8-child review in minutes / lower token cost", "no fidelity regression (offline-eval tolerance)", and "make lint + full suite pass" cannot be verified at the epic-close grain. S4 carries live, unresolved G4 container-review findings (per-child guardrail ownership split). Substantial S1/S3/S6 code HAS landed (runner.py:421 _anthropic_cache_settings; pass1.py warm-then-fan-out; eval_specs/plan-review-container.eval.yaml), but completion is determined by signed child-ticket CLOSURE, not code presence. Recommendation: drive S1/S2/S3/S6 to signed close, implement+close S4/S5, then re-verify the epic-level wall-clock/token/fidelity/lint criteria.
